agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A logistics system with an established domain model governing Shipment-Carrier assignment. The product team has requested an 'auto-split' feature that would automatically divide overweight shipments across multiple carriers, bypassing supervisor approval.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management / Carrier Assignment","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A unit of freight to be transported, which must have exactly one assigned Carrier."},{"term":"Carrier","definition":"The transport provider assigned to fulfill a Shipment."},{"term":"Sub-shipment","definition":"A proposed new concept: a child Shipment created by splitting a parent Shipment that exceeds a weight threshold."},{"term":"Carrier Assignment","definition":"The binding of exactly one Carrier to a Shipment. Once set, only a Supervisor can change it."},{"term":"Supervisor","definition":"A privileged role that is the sole authority permitted to reassign a Carrier on a Shipment."},{"term":"Auto-split","definition":"Proposed behavior where the system automatically divides a Shipment exceeding 500 kg into multiple sub-shipments, each with a different Carrier, without Supervisor involvement."},{"term":"Dispatch","definition":"The point at which a Shipment is sent out for delivery. The system guarantees every Shipment has exactly one Carrier at this moment."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A Shipment must have exactly one assigned Carrier.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a state integrity constraint. A Shipment with zero or multiple Carriers is in a corrupt state — accountability, tracking, billing, and liability all depend on a single Carrier per Shipment.","testable_assertion":"For every Shipment in any state, COUNT(assigned_carriers) == 1."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Once a Carrier is assigned to a Shipment, only a Supervisor can reassign it.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is an authorization invariant protecting assignment integrity. Without it, uncontrolled reassignment creates liability gaps, audit failures, and operational chaos.","testable_assertion":"Any Carrier reassignment operation must include a valid Supervisor authorization; otherwise it is rejected."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"At the time of dispatch, every Shipment has exactly one Carrier.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a physical and contractual constraint — a shipment leaves the facility under exactly one carrier's responsibility. Violating this means no single party is accountable for the freight in transit.","testable_assertion":"The dispatch operation must fail if the Shipment does not have exactly one assigned Carrier."}],"acceptance_criteria":[],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"A Shipment of exactly 500 kg — does it split or not?","expected_behavior":"Undefined in current request. Boundary must be clarified (> 500 vs >= 500)."},{"scenario":"A Shipment is already assigned to a Carrier and then its weight is updated to exceed 500 kg.","expected_behavior":"Auto-split would need to remove the existing Carrier assignment without Supervisor involvement, directly violating BR-2."},{"scenario":"A sub-shipment itself exceeds 500 kg after splitting.","expected_behavior":"Recursive splitting? The request does not address this."},{"scenario":"Parent Shipment tracking after split — does the original Shipment cease to exist?","expected_behavior":"If the parent Shipment still exists with no Carrier or multiple Carriers, BR-1 is violated. If it is voided, downstream references (customer tracking, invoices) break."},{"scenario":"One sub-shipment's Carrier fails delivery but others succeed.","expected_behavior":"Partial delivery semantics are undefined. The original Shipment concept assumes atomic delivery by one Carrier."},{"scenario":"Billing and liability split across multiple Carriers for what was a single customer order.","expected_behavior":"Undefined. SLAs, insurance claims, and invoicing all assume one Carrier per Shipment."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Splitting shipments across carriers changes billing models, may increase total freight cost due to loss of volume discounts, and creates complex liability allocation for damage claims.","operational":"Introduces a new 'sub-shipment' aggregate that the entire system (tracking, dispatch, delivery confirmation, returns) must understand. Significant operational complexity increase.","user":"Customers tracking a single shipment would now see multiple sub-shipments with different carriers and potentially different delivery times. This fragments the customer experience.","risk":"HIGH. The feature as described directly contradicts two production invariants (BR-1 one-carrier-per-shipment and BR-2 supervisor-only reassignment). Shipping it as specified would corrupt the domain model.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Operations / Dispatch","impact":"Must handle multi-carrier coordination for what was previously a single-carrier workflow."},{"group":"Supervisors","impact":"Their authority over carrier assignment is bypassed, undermining the control structure that exists for accountability reasons."},{"group":"Carriers","impact":"Now receive partial shipments, affecting their load planning, pricing, and contractual obligations."},{"group":"Customers","impact":"A single order may arrive in multiple deliveries from different carriers, complicating tracking and receipt."},{"group":"Finance / Billing","impact":"Must split invoicing, reconcile partial deliveries, and allocate liability across multiple carriers."},{"group":"Legal / Compliance","impact":"Chain of custody, insurance, and liability frameworks assume one carrier per shipment. Splitting without legal review creates exposure."}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"The feature request directly contradicts two established production invariants (one-carrier-per-shipment and supervisor-only reassignment). This is not an ambiguity — it is a clear contradiction between the requested behavior and the existing domain model. Confidence is low because the spec as written cannot be implemented without either breaking invariants or fundamentally redesigning the domain model, and the request provides no guidance on which path to take.","high_confidence_areas":["The contradiction between auto-split and BR-1/BR-2 is unambiguous","The one-Carrier-per-Shipment invariant is a true invariant, not a soft policy"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether the product team intends to relax the existing invariants or wants a design that preserves them","Whether 'sub-shipment' is meant to be a first-class Shipment or a new aggregate","Billing, liability, and tracking implications have not been addressed"],"assumptions":["The existing domain model and invariants are correctly stated and currently enforced in production","The product team may not have been aware of the invariant conflicts when writing this request"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"The auto-split feature directly violates BR-1 (one Carrier per Shipment). If a Shipment is split into sub-shipments each handled by a different Carrier, either: (a) the sub-shipments are Shipments, in which case the original Shipment's Carrier assignment is destroyed, or (b) they are not Shipments, in which case a new aggregate type is introduced that the entire system does not yet understand.","affected_stakeholders":["Operations","Customers","Finance","Carriers"],"options":["Reject the feature as incompatible with the current domain model","Introduce a new 'ShipmentGroup' or 'MultiCarrierShipment' aggregate where each sub-shipment is a full Shipment with its own single Carrier, and the parent becomes a coordination entity (not a Shipment)","Relax BR-1 to allow multiple Carriers per Shipment (major domain model change requiring full impact analysis)"],"recommendation":"Introduce a 'ShipmentGroup' aggregate that owns multiple child Shipments. Each child Shipment preserves BR-1 (exactly one Carrier). The parent ShipmentGroup is a new concept, not a Shipment, so no invariant is violated. This requires explicit domain modeling work."},{"type":"contradiction","description":"The auto-split feature bypasses Supervisor authority for carrier assignment (BR-2). The request says splitting happens 'without requiring supervisor involvement,' but BR-2 states only a Supervisor can change carrier assignments. If the original Shipment had a Carrier and is now split, reassignment occurs without Supervisor approval.","affected_stakeholders":["Supervisors","Operations","Legal/Compliance"],"options":["Require Supervisor approval for auto-split (contradicts 'automatic' requirement but preserves BR-2)","Treat auto-split as initial assignment of new Shipments rather than reassignment (preserves BR-2 if the original Shipment is voided by a Supervisor)","Relax BR-2 for system-initiated splits specifically (policy carve-out, requires Supervisor stakeholder buy-in)"],"recommendation":"Auto-split should be treated as the creation of new Shipments (not reassignment), and the voiding/cancellation of the original Shipment should require Supervisor authorization. This preserves the Supervisor's authority while enabling the feature. The 'automatic' part is the proposal and sub-shipment creation; the Supervisor approves the split."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Auto-split assigns multiple Carriers to what is logically one Shipment, violating the one-Carrier-per-Shipment invariant.","business_rule_broken":"BR-1"},{"violation":"Auto-split bypasses Supervisor authority for carrier assignment changes, violating the Supervisor-only reassignment invariant.","business_rule_broken":"BR-2"}]}
